name: Boston Cream Pie2
recipeIngredient: Chocolate (3 ounces, semisweet or milk chocolate)
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the cake layers: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease and flour two 9-inch round cake pans.
2. In a large bowl with an electric mixer on medium speed, beat together butter, sugar, eggs, milk, and vanilla until thoroughly blended and smooth. Pour batter into prepared pans.
3. Bake for 30 minutes or until a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out clean. Remove from pans and let cool completely on wire racks.
4. Prepare the custard: In a 2-quart saucepan, stir together sugar, cornstarch, and salt. Gradually add milk while stirring constantly until smooth. Cook over medium heat, stirring frequently, until boiling rapidly and thickening for about 1 minute.
5. Remove from heat, then slowly pour in lightly beaten egg yolks, stirring rapidly to blend and keep the mixture smooth. Return to low heat and cook for an additional 1 minute without boiling. Stir in vanilla and let cool completely.
6. Prepare the glaze: In a small heavy saucepan over very low heat, melt chocolate with butter, stirring frequently until blended. Add confectioners' sugar and milk, stirring until smooth. Keep warm and covered.
7. Assemble: Carefully cut cooled cake layers in half horizontally using a sharp serrated knife. Place one layer, cut side up, on a serving platter. Spread with about 1/3 of the custard. Repeat with remaining layers, ending with the bottom layer cut side down.
8. Drizzle warm glaze over the top and let it drip down the sides. Makes 12 to 16 servings.
